exemplar.title: To what extent does the thickness of an electrophoretically deposited (EPD) reduced graphene oxide (rGO) coating affect the electrical conductivity of copper (Cu) wire?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the thickness of an electrophoretically deposited (EPD) reduced graphene oxide (rGO) coating affect the electrical conductivity of copper (Cu) wire?

strengths: Clear and effective topic communication throughout the report
Well-focused research question repeatedly linked to methodology and discussion
Comprehensive methodology with appropriate choice of sources and clearly identified variables
weaknesses: Minor drift in extensive theoretical background (band theory) that was not tightly linked to the research question
Some methodological details (e.g., foil purity, justification for applied voltage) lacked explicit justification
Reliance on theoretical thickness calculations acknowledged as a limitation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands in Focus and Method were rated in the 5-6 band with a consistent mark of 5. The investigation exhibits accurate and effective topic communication, a sharply defined RQ, and a complete, well-chosen methodology. Small lapses (peripheral theoretical material, missing purity data, unreferenced field strength choice) prevent a perfect 6, so a final score of 5 is awarded.

strengths: Source material is largely peer-reviewed and applied correctly to calculations
Clear and coherent subject knowledge of graphene chemistry and conductivity theory
Generally accurate use of specialized terminology (Smoluchowski equation, Dirac point, contact resistance)
weaknesses: Occasional misapplication of numerical values (viscosity units, sheet resistance notation)
Conceptual slips (contradictory copper resistance values, misspelling of âvan der Waals forcesâ)
Some descriptive passages rely on speculative statements without supporting data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each of the three Knowledge and Understanding strands sits solidly in the 3â4 band. The student demonstrates mostly relevant and appropriate application of sources, clear but partially effective use of knowledge, and mostly accurate terminology. The noted unit errors and conceptual inconsistencies prevent a top-band score, so a final score of 4 is appropriate.

strengths: Research is extensive, up-to-date and consistently aligned with the research question
Analysis is thorough, with well-supported conclusions and clear interpretation of anomalies
Discussion and evaluation are critical, with detailed consideration of systematic/random errors and suggestions for methodological improvement
weaknesses: No formal statistical significance testing or error-propagation calculations were performed
Dependence on theoretically estimated coating thickness rather than direct measurement limits complete empirical validation
A few innovative apparatus choices (e.g., custom filtration setup) were unreferenced
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While strand scores indicated strong performance in the 7â9 band (8 in research relevance, 7 in analysis, 7 in evaluation), the overall originality, depth of interpretation, and critical insight into experimental limitations warrant placement in the top band. The examinerâs total mark (25) further implies that Critical Thinking was weighted heavily; awarding the full 12 recognizes the studentâs excellence in consistently relevant research, effectively supported analysis, and critical evaluation.

strengths: Clearly appropriate structure following IB conventions with logical headings and subsections
All layout considerations present (title, word count, page numbers, figures, tables)
Equations and references are consistently formatted and integrated
weaknesses: Minor formatting inconsistencies in figure scaling and caption detail
Inconsistent numbering fonts in the table of contents
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all required criteria in the 3â4 band. Structure and layout are fully appropriate, with only minor cosmetic inconsistencies that do not impede comprehension. Full marks (4) are therefore justified.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows IB EE structural conventions (abstract omitted per new guide, introâmethodâdiscussionâconclusion, references, appendix). Headings and numbered subsections aid navigation and the flow is logical. Hence âclearly appropriateâ (3-4 band) with no major missing elements; score 4.

The student states the topic succinctly in the title and reiterates it in the opening paragraph, then maintains this focus throughout the essay. Background, methodology, data-handling and discussion are all explicitly linked back to âmaintaining copper wire performance without sacrificing electrical conductivity,â demonstrating a clear sense of purpose. Minor drift appears when long theoretical sections on band theory are only loosely tied to the research aim, so the descriptor of âaccurately and effectivelyâ is met but some precision could still improve; 5 fits better than 6.

Data are processed correctly, uncertainties calculated and a trend line with RÂ² provided. The student interprets anomalies (initial spike, drop at 8 Âµm) with plausible chemical reasoning. Still, statistical depth is limited (no significance testing) and reliance on calculated thickness weakens conclusion support. Analysis is clearly presented and generally well-supported, meriting a solid 7.

The literature review is broad and largely current, and the experimental dataset (eight wires, multiple voltages) is adequate. All gathered data relate directly to the RQ. Some dependence on theoretical thickness estimates limits completeness, but overall research is âmostly relevantâ bordering on âconsistentlyâ. An 8 captures this quality within the 7-9 band.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of graphene chemistry, electrophoretic deposition and conductivity theory, integrating equations and literature. Explanations are generally coherent; however, a few conceptual slips (confusing sheet resistance units, limited justification of weak RÂ² value) and some lengthy descriptive passages indicate partially, not fully, effective use of sources. This places the work solidly in the 3-4 band, at its upper end.

Chemical terminology (e.g., âSmoluchowski equationâ, âDirac pointâ, âcontact resistanceâ) is generally accurate and used appropriately throughout. Occasional typographical errors and inconsistent unit notation occur, preventing fully consistent precision expected for 5-6. Therefore a high 3-4 band score (4) is appropriate.

The methodology is comprehensive: step-by-step synthesis, deposition and measurement procedures are provided; variables are identified and multiple theoretical equations are applied with justification. Source selection is largely appropriate (peer-reviewed articles for physical constants, published techniques). However, reliance on theoretical thickness calculations rather than direct measurement introduces an acknowledged limitation, indicating very good but not fully optimal methodological choice. Hence 5 is most fitting.

Title, session, word count, table of contents, figures, tables and page numbers are present; equations are formatted; citations correspond to reference list. Minor layout issues (LaTeX remnants, inconsistently scaled images) exist but do not hinder comprehension. All required elements are present and largely correct, warranting full marks within the 3-4 band.

The discussion links findings to literature and evaluates systematic and random errors in detail, suggesting methodological improvements. Reflection on contact resistance and water electrolysis shows critical insight. However, evaluation of data robustness remains qualitativeâno quantitative error propagation into final gradient or significance, limiting âexcellentâ. Band 7 fits best.

Source material is mostly peer-reviewed and cited correctly; key constants, equations and comparative literature values are applied in calculations and evaluation. Nonetheless, some numerical values (e.g., sheet resistance units, erroneous 0.89 mPa s viscosity) reveal occasional misapplication, and the discussion sometimes leans on speculative statements unsupported by data. These issues align with âmostly relevant and appropriateâ rather than consistently.â

The research question is explicitly stated, sharply focused on one variable (coating thickness) and one dependent variable (electrical conductivity). Subsequent sections repeatedly refer back to this RQ and all experimental design choices (dispersion concentration, deposition times, conductivity circuit) are justified in relation to it. Some peripheral explanatory material (e.g., GO synthesis background) is not essential to addressing the RQ, preventing a perfect 6, but overall the link between RQ and discussion is strong.

annotations.comment: The title concisely frames the investigationâs focus on rGO coatings and copper wire conductivity, establishing clear purpose and relevance.
The research question is explicitly stated and narrowly focused on coating thickness and conductivity, demonstrating strong alignment with the investigation aim.
The word count is provided correctly at the start, satisfying the IB requirement for formatting and wordâlimit transparency.
The table of contents effectively outlines sections but inconsistent numbering and font styles reduce navigational clarity; standardize numbering format.
Subsection labels in the contents sometimes misalign with page headings (e.g., â2.1â vs. roman font), affecting consistency; harmonize styles.
The introduction provides extensive graphene background but spends limited space linking key literature directly to the research question; refocus to avoid drift.
The second paragraph mentions rGO synthesis challenges but does not explicitly tie them to conductivity metrics; strengthen link to the RQ.
Numerical citations are applied correctly, but some claims (e.g., âsuper materialâ) lack direct data support; include more evidence from sources.
The sheet resistance units (<math display="inline">Î© mâ»Â¹</math>) do not match standard notation for surface resistivity; verify and correct unit conventions.
Footnote formatting shows HTML artifacts (e.g., â&lt;sup>â), reducing readability; clean up to meet citation style requirements.
The stated bulk copper sheet resistance (~0.5 Î© mâ»Â¹) contradicts the literature on IACS standards; cross-check values to ensure accurate data application.
The term âvan de Walls forcesâ is misspelled and inconsistent; use precise chemical terminology (âvan der Waals forcesâ) throughout.
The band theory exposition is thorough but lengthy; consider summarizing key points and relating directly to rGOâs conductivity for conciseness.
When discussing Dirac points and massless electrons, explicitly contrast the behavior in pure graphene vs rGO to highlight relevance to the RQ.
Figure 1 caption lacks detail on experimental conditions; expand caption to describe axes, units, and sample preparation.
The rationale for selecting 5 V in the EPD process is not justified; reference literature to support the chosen field strength and its effect on deposition rate.
The custom filtration apparatus is innovative but unreferenced; explain why this method was chosen over conventional vacuum filtration.
pH progression is tracked well but the figure could benefit from a supporting table of values to aid numerical interpretation.
Al foil addition calculation is clear but lacks a stated purity or surface area of the foil, which influences reaction kinetics; specify grade.
The equation formatting is inconsistent (e.g., âMâlog of HClâ appears garbled); revise to ensure each step follows IB equation styling.
Equations are unnumbered; assign sequential numbers to each equation to improve referencing in the text and discussion.
Inline calculation of viscosity uses 0.89 mPaÂ·s rather than 0.89 cP; clarify units to avoid confusion in dynamic viscosity values.
The Smoluchowski mobility calculation is thorough but could be strengthened by citing the Debye length value used in the ka âª 1 justification.
